ucs.title: [LLM] APT32 (OceanLotus) DLL side-loading via vendor-signed host binaries (rastlsc.exe / DTHelper.exe / wsc_proxy.exe)
ucs.description: Hunts the hallmark OceanLotus / Cobalt Kitty staging chain: a legitimate vendor-signed binary (Symantec rastlsc.exe, McAfee mcoemcpy.exe, Sophos/Adobe DTHelper.exe, Avast wsc_proxy.exe, Google goopdate.exe) executed from a user-writeable directory so it side-loads an attacker DLL of the same expected name (rastls.dll, mcutil.dll, goopdate.dll). Cybereason and FireEye/Mandiant have repeatedly attributed this exact set of side-load hosts to APT32.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as command values(Processes.process_path) as process_path values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent_process_name from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("rastlsc.exe","RasTlsc.exe","RasTls.exe","mcoemcpy.exe","DTHelper.exe","wsc_proxy.exe","goopdate.exe","mcvsshld.exe","AcroBroker.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where NOT match(process_path, "(?i)^[A-Z]:\\\\Program Files( \\(x86\\))?\\\\") AND NOT match(process_path, "(?i)^[A-Z]:\\\\Windows\\\\") | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
let APT32_SideloadHosts = dynamic(["rastlsc.exe","rastls.exe","mcoemcpy.exe","dthelper.exe","wsc_proxy.exe","goopdate.exe","mcvsshld.exe","acrobroker.exe"]);
let APT32_SideloadDlls = dynamic(["rastls.dll","mcutil.dll","goopdate.dll","dwmapi.dll","wsc.dll"]);
// Stage A — host binary executes from user-writeable path (already a strong tell)
let StageA = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where FileName in~ (APT32_SideloadHosts)
    | where FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Program Files"
         and FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Program Files (x86)"
         and FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Windows\"
    | project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              HostBinary = FileName, HostBinaryPath = FolderPath,
              HostBinarySHA256 = SHA256, HostCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
              ParentName = InitiatingProcessFileName, ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
// Stage B — same host binary loads its expected sideload DLL from the same non-standard dir
let StageB = DeviceImageLoadEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (APT32_SideloadHosts)
    | where FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Program Files"
         and FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Program Files (x86)"
         and FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Windows\"
    | where FolderPath has_any (@"\AppData\", @"\Temp\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\Roaming\")
    | where FileName in~ (APT32_SideloadDlls) or FolderPath =~ InitiatingProcessFolderPath
    | project LoadTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, LoadedDll = FileName, LoadedDllPath = FolderPath,
              LoadedDllSHA256 = SHA256, HostBinaryPath = InitiatingProcessFolderPath;
StageA
| join kind=inner StageB on DeviceId, HostBinaryPath
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, HostBinary, HostBinaryPath,
          HostBinarySHA256, HostCmd, LoadedDll, LoadedDllPath, LoadedDllSHA256,
          ParentName, ParentCmd
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let APT32_SideloadHosts = dynamic(["rastlsc.exe","rastls.exe","mcoemcpy.exe","dthelper.exe","wsc_proxy.exe","goopdate.exe","mcvsshld.exe","acrobroker.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend ImageName = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where ImageName in~ (APT32_SideloadHosts)
// signed vendor binaries should ONLY ever execute from their install dirs
| where NewProcessName !startswith @"C:\Program Files\"
     and NewProcessName !startswith @"C:\Program Files (x86)\"
     and NewProcessName !startswith @"C:\Windows\"
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, ImageName, NewProcessName,
          CommandLine, ParentProcessName, TokenElevationType, IpAddress
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT32 OceanLotus DLL Side-loading via Abused Vendor-Signed Binaries
id: a1b2c3d4-e5f6-4789-abcd-ef0123456789
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects APT32 (OceanLotus / Cobalt Kitty) staging where a vendor-signed binary
  known to be abused by the actor for side-loading (rastlsc.exe, mcoemcpy.exe,
  DTHelper.exe, wsc_proxy.exe, goopdate.exe) loads a DLL from a user-writeable
  directory (\AppData, \Temp, \ProgramData, \Roaming, \Users\Public).
references:
  - https://www.cybereason.com/blog/operation-cobalt-kitty-apt
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0050/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/07
logsource:
  category: image_load
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_image:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\rastlsc.exe'
      - '\rastls.exe'
      - '\mcoemcpy.exe'
      - '\DTHelper.exe'
      - '\wsc_proxy.exe'
      - '\goopdate.exe'
      - '\mcvsshld.exe'
      - '\AcroBroker.exe'
  selection_userwriteable:
    ImageLoaded|contains:
      - '\AppData\'
      - '\Temp\'
      - '\ProgramData\'
      - '\Users\Public\'
      - '\Roaming\'
  filter_legit:
    ImageLoaded|startswith:
      - 'C:\Program Files\'
      - 'C:\Program Files (x86)\'
      - 'C:\Windows\'
  condition: selection_image and selection_userwriteable and not filter_legit
falsepositives:
  - Portable / per-user installs of legitimate Symantec, McAfee, Sophos, Avast or Google updater products (rare in enterprise)
level: high
tags:
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1574.002
  - attack.t1036.005
  - attack.g0050
ucs.rationale: These specific signed-vendor binaries (rastlsc.exe, mcoemcpy.exe, DTHelper.exe, wsc_proxy.exe, goopdate.exe) are not generic LOLBins — they are specifically the side-load hosts documented in the Cybereason 'Operation Cobalt Kitty' APT32 report and Mandiant's OceanLotus reporting. Legitimate vendor installations only ever drop these binaries into their canonical Program Files paths; observing them

ucs.title: [LLM] APT32 (OceanLotus) COM hijacking persistence — InprocServer32 pointing into user-writeable directory
ucs.description: Hunts COM Object hijacking persistence as documented in Cybereason's Cobalt Kitty deep-dive on APT32: the actor writes HKCU\Software\Classes\CLSID\{GUID}\InprocServer32 with a Default value pointing at a malicious DLL staged in AppData/Temp/ProgramData. Because writes happen under HKCU they require no elevation — perfect for OceanLotus's non-admin foothold and they survive reboots without a Run-key alert.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Registry.registry_value_name) as registry_value_name values(Registry.registry_value_data) as registry_value_data values(Registry.process_name) as process_name values(Registry.process_path) as process_path from datamodel=Endpoint.Registry where Registry.registry_path="*\\Software\\Classes\\CLSID\\*\\InprocServer32*" AND (Registry.registry_value_data="*\\AppData\\*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*\\Temp\\*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*\\Roaming\\*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*\\Users\\Public\\*") by Registry.dest Registry.user Registry.registry_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Registry)` | where NOT match(process_name, "(?i)^(msiexec\\.exe|trustedinstaller\\.exe|setup\\.exe|.+_setup\\.exe|installer\\.exe)$") | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
// APT32 / Cobalt Kitty signature: HKCU CLSID InprocServer32 -> user-writeable DLL
let APT32_KnownHijackedCLSIDs = dynamic([
    "{B5F8350B-0548-48B1-A6EE-88BD00B4A5E7}",   // MruObjects (Cobalt Kitty)
    "{0EE63A2B-2E48-4DEA-BC14-6F0FE25406BD}",   // CLSID hijacked in OceanLotus telemetry
    "{42aedc87-2188-41fd-b9a3-0c966feabec1}",
    "{16d51579-a30b-4c8b-a276-0ff4dc41e755}"
]);
DeviceRegistryEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where ActionType in ("RegistryValueSet","RegistryKeyCreated")
| where RegistryKey has @"\Software\Classes\CLSID\"
     and RegistryKey has @"InprocServer32"
| where RegistryValueName in~ ("","(Default)","ThreadingModel")
| where RegistryValueData has_any (@"\AppData\", @"\Temp\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Roaming\", @"\Users\Public\")
   or RegistryValueData endswith ".dll"
   and RegistryValueData !startswith @"C:\Windows\"
   and RegistryValueData !startswith @"C:\Program Files"
// Drop legit installer noise
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("msiexec.exe","trustedinstaller.exe","setup.exe","installer.exe","omadmclient.exe")
| extend CLSID = extract(@"CLSID\\(\{[A-Fa-f0-9\-]+\})", 1, RegistryKey)
| extend KnownAPT32Clsid = iif(CLSID in~ (APT32_KnownHijackedCLSIDs), "YES", "NO")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          CLSID, KnownAPT32Clsid,
          RegistryKey, RegistryValueName, RegistryValueData
| order by KnownAPT32Clsid desc, Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let APT32_KnownHijackedCLSIDs = dynamic([
    "{B5F8350B-0548-48B1-A6EE-88BD00B4A5E7}",
    "{0EE63A2B-2E48-4DEA-BC14-6F0FE25406BD}",
    "{42aedc87-2188-41fd-b9a3-0c966feabec1}",
    "{16d51579-a30b-4c8b-a276-0ff4dc41e755}"
]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4657   // registry value modified — requires registry auditing
| where ObjectName has @"\Software\Classes\CLSID\"
     and ObjectName has @"InprocServer32"
| where ObjectValueName in~ ("","(Default)","ThreadingModel")
| extend NewValue = tostring(extract(@"<Data Name=""NewValue"">([^<]+)</Data>", 1, EventData))
| where isnotempty(NewValue)
| where NewValue has_any (@"\AppData\", @"\Temp\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Roaming\", @"\Users\Public\")
     or (NewValue endswith ".dll"
         and NewValue !startswith @"C:\Windows\"
         and NewValue !startswith @"C:\Program Files")
| where SubjectUserName !endswith "$"
| extend CLSID = extract(@"CLSID\\(\{[A-Fa-f0-9\-]+\})", 1, ObjectName)
| extend KnownAPT32Clsid = iif(CLSID in~ (APT32_KnownHijackedCLSIDs), "YES", "NO")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, SubjectUserName, ProcessName,
          CLSID, KnownAPT32Clsid, ObjectName, ObjectValueName, NewValue
| order by KnownAPT32Clsid desc, TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT32 OceanLotus COM Hijacking via HKCU InprocServer32 to User-Writeable Path
id: c3d4e5f6-a7b8-4901-cdef-2345678901ab
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects APT32 (OceanLotus / Cobalt Kitty) persistence via Component Object Model
  hijacking — writing a DLL path under HKCU\Software\Classes\CLSID\{GUID}\InprocServer32
  whose value points into a user-writeable directory (\AppData, \Temp, \ProgramData,
  \Roaming, \Users\Public).
references:
  - https://www.cybereason.com/blog/operation-cobalt-kitty-apt
  - https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1546/015/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0050/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/07
logsource:
  category: registry_set
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_path:
    TargetObject|contains: '\Software\Classes\CLSID\'
    TargetObject|contains|all:
      - 'InprocServer32'
  selection_value:
    Details|contains:
      - '\AppData\'
      - '\Temp\'
      - '\ProgramData\'
      - '\Roaming\'
      - '\Users\Public\'
  filter_installer:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\msiexec.exe'
      - '\TrustedInstaller.exe'
      - '\setup.exe'
      - '\installer.exe'
  condition: selection_path and selection_value and not filter_installer
falsepositives:
  - Per-user installers writing COM registrations under HKCU (rare and usually parented to msiexec.exe)
level: high
tags:
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.privilege_escalation
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1546.015
  - attack.t1112
  - attack.g0050
ucs.rationale: COM hijacking via HKCU\Software\Classes\CLSID\{GUID}\InprocServer32 was the signature persistence mechanism described in Cybereason's 'Operation Cobalt Kitty' APT32 report — the actor specifically targeted CLSIDs like MruObjects ({B5F8350B-...}) so that user-mode COM consumers (Explorer, Office) auto-load the staged DLL on next launch. A near-zero-baseline signal exists for HKCU CLSID InprocServer
